title: Skill prose wiring: swarming dispatches computed waves; validating checks the schedule; files-authoring note
action: Per D1/D2: (1) bee-swarming SKILL.md wave-analysis step (line ~32): replace the manual walk with 'run node .bee/bin/bee.mjs cells schedule --json; the computed waves are the default dispatch order — override only with a stated reason recorded in the swarm report; refuse to dispatch when diagnostics report cycles'. Keep the existing doctrine sentence that two overlapping cells are never spawned together. (2) bee-validating SKILL.md feasibility matrix bullet: add a schedule row — 'bee cells schedule reports zero cycles and expected wave shape' as required evidence for multi-cell slices. (3) planning-reference.md files-authoring guidance (~line 106): add the note that cross-cell file overlap is legal but costs a wave (auto-serialized per D2), and files entries should prefer explicit paths or trailing-* patterns because overlap uses pathsOverlap semantics (D3). Keep each edit minimal and in the file's existing voice.

truths: swarming prose names the exact command and the override-with-stated-reason rule (D1)
no prose contradicts the write-guard deny safety net (D2 keeps it)
planning prose states overlap-is-legal-costs-a-wave and the trailing-* preference (D3)
artifacts: edited sections in the three files
prohibitions: editing installed copies under .claude/skills or .agents/skills (onboarding sync owns propagation)

verify: rg -q "cells schedule" skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md && rg -q "cells schedule" skills/bee-validating/SKILL.md && rg -q "costs a wave" skills/bee-planning/references/planning-reference.md && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs

trace.outcome: Wired skill prose to the computed schedule (D1/D2/D3): bee-swarming's wave-analysis step now runs 'bee cells schedule --json' as the default dispatch order with a stated-reason override and cycle refusal; bee-validating's feasibility matrix requires a schedule row (zero cycles, expected wave shape) for multi-cell slices; planning-reference.md's Bounded files rule notes overlap is legal-costs-a-wave and prefers explicit/trailing-* paths.
